nodes.label: Design decision: masked-feature reconstruction as the decided prediction target
nodes.description: Decided (v2 of the design): a flat-MLP layer predicts input dimensions masked out of it (per-layer denoising). Chosen because it dissolves 'flat vectors have no structure to predict,' is fully single-sample-pure, directly cures information loss, is a one-line goodness reframe, and has the strongest existence proof (greedy layer-wise denoising AEs, Hinton'06/Bengio'07).
nodes.rationale: Sibling-contrastive (CLAPP) kept only as the control, since it needs negatives/augmentation (less single-sample-pure).

nodes.label: The Phase-3 levers table
nodes.description: masked-feature reconstruction (denoising-AE, single-sample-pure), sibling-contrastive (CLAPP, InfoNCE, needs negatives), cross-layer goodness (OLU/DF-O, the author's Direction 1, 2-layer window), direct feedback (top-down broadcast), Mono-Forward (supervised per-layer CE fallback).

nodes.label: Greedy InfoMax / CLAPP existence proof
nodes.description: Forward-only, gradient-isolated, unsupervised local learners (GIM, CLAPP) whose per-layer accuracy RISES with depth because the objective is information-preserving/predictive (InfoNCE), not energy. A Jan-2026 benchmark (2601.21683) tests SCFF by name and shows the predictive local-SSL family matches end-to-end backprop-SSL on CIFAR-10.
nodes.rationale: The literature lever Phase 2 never touched -- the existence proof that motivated the entire Phase-3 objective reframe.
